reasoning: XLK down -1.35% is the dominant headwind; tech represents ~30% of SPY and is underperforming. Simultaneously XLE up +1.25% and XLF up +0.83% reveals defensive rotation: growth capital exiting into defensive sectors (energy) and financials. VIX rising +8.48% confirms underlying volatility concern. SPY down -0.57% reflects tech's drag outweighing financial+energy support. Sector breadth is narrow (only defensives participating), confirming classic risk-off setup. Medium-high confidence.
key_observations: XLK: -1.35% tech sell-off is the critical headwind driving SPY lower; growth leadership absent.
XLE: +1.25% energy up on defensive demand — investors rotating from growth into safety.
XLF: +0.83% financials modest support, but insufficient to overcome tech drag.
Overall rotation: Risk-off confirmed — growth exiting to defensive, SPY following tech down.
